name: Miso Marinade for Fish
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup miso (light, dark, whatever you got)
4-6 fish steaks, about 3/4 inch thick, 5-6 oz per steak (I used halibut)
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine miso, water, and mirin in a small saucepan. Heat over moderate-low flame for about 5 minutes. Stir occasionally to dissolve the miso.
2. Pour into a baking dish large enough to hold all the fish steaks in one layer. Pat the fish steaks dry. Place them in the baking dish. Let sit for 15 minutes. Turn over and marinate for at least 6 hours in the refrigerator. You can marinate it overnight if you want. Turn once or twice.
3. Preheat the grill or broiler. Remove fish from marinade. Grill steaks for 3 minutes per side, about. Place on serving platter, sprinkle with sesame seeds and black pepper. Serve.
Note: Mirin is sweet rice wine. Look for it in the supermarket with other Asian ingredients or at an Asian market.
